prompt: The metadata for the flask-apcore demo at /Users/tercelyi/Workspace/aipartnerup/flask-apcore/examples/demo/ is pretty bare-bones. Can you improve the create_task.post and delete_task.delete modules? Read the source code in app.py to understand what these functions actually do, then generate better descriptions, parameter descriptions, documentation, examples, tags, and the x-when-to-use metadata. Show me the changes before applying anything.

assertions.text: The agent read app.py source code to understand function behavior
Changes are shown as a diff/comparison BEFORE any files are written to the original location
Parameter names in input_schema are preserved exactly (title, description, done, task_id) — not renamed
Module descriptions are improved beyond the originals ('Create a new task.' and 'Delete a task permanently.')
input_schema properties get 'description' fields (e.g., title, description, done for create_task; task_id for delete_task)
At least 1 example per module with realistic data (not 'test' or 'foo')
Tags are generated (should include 'tasks' or similar domain tags)
x-when-to-use metadata is generated for both modules
Annotations are accurate: create_task should NOT be destructive, delete_task should be destructive
